title: Add Super Admin recovery flow and make Super Admin role initialization reliable

requirements.text: Persist Super Admin identity and initialization state across canister upgrades so Super Admin access does not get lost after rebuilds/updates.

quotes: fix it so that I can access the super admin make another way

acceptanceCriteria: After a canister upgrade, the previously-initialized Super Admin Principal remains the Super Admin without requiring re-initialization.
Role checks (e.g., getCallerRole) continue to return "SuperAdmin" for the persisted Super Admin Principal after upgrade.

requirements.text: Add a deterministic, authenticated Super Admin recovery/initialization API that allows regaining Super Admin access without relying on the user finding/copying a Principal ID from external tools. The flow must be safe-by-default and must not allow anonymous callers to become Super Admin.

quotes: fix it so that I can access the super admin make another way

acceptanceCriteria: When called by an authenticated (non-anonymous) user on an uninitialized canister, the recovery/initialization API sets that caller as Super Admin and subsequent getCallerRole returns "SuperAdmin".
When called on an already-initialized canister by a non-Super-Admin, the API does not change the stored Super Admin and returns an error (or otherwise denies the action).
Anonymous callers cannot use this API to become Super Admin.

requirements.text: Update the Admin Dashboard UI (frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboardPage.tsx) to always show the currently logged-in Principal ID (from the Internet Identity identity object) with a copy-to-clipboard control, so the user can reliably identify their Principal even if backend role/permissions are temporarily "Unknown".

quotes: fix it so that I can access the super admin make another way

acceptanceCriteria: When logged in, the Admin Dashboard shows a "Principal ID" line containing the full principal text.
A user can copy the Principal ID via a visible UI control (e.g., button) and receives a clear success indication (e.g., "Copied").
This Principal ID display renders regardless of whether the backend role query returns "Unknown"/errors.

requirements.text: Add a "Super Admin Recovery" section on the Admin Dashboard that provides an alternate way to regain access by triggering the new backend recovery/initialization API and clearly communicating outcomes in English (success/error). Show this section only when the user is logged in but does not currently have Super Admin access.

quotes: fix it so that I can access the super admin make another way

acceptanceCriteria: If the logged-in user is not Super Admin, the Admin Dashboard shows a "Super Admin Recovery" section.
Clicking a recovery button runs the recovery/initialization action and, on success, the dashboard updates to show Role: SuperAdmin and unlocks Super Admin-only cards.
If the canister is already initialized to a different Super Admin, the UI shows a clear English error message explaining that only the current Super Admin can change Super Admin access.

requirements.text: Make the post-login admin experience reliably resolve the correct role by ensuring a role-initializing shared call is executed after a successful Internet Identity login (so the system does not remain stuck showing role "Unknown"/"Guest" due to only query calls being made).

quotes: fix it so that I can access the super admin make another way

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in via the Header "Admin Login" flow, the Admin Dashboard consistently shows the correct role (e.g., SuperAdmin/AdminUser/User) without requiring manual refreshes.
The UI no longer commonly displays transient "Unknown" role due to missing initialization calls when the canister is not yet initialized.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/immutablePaths (e.g., frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/components/ui).
Backend must remain single-actor; keep Motoko logic in backend/main.mo. Create backend/migration.mo only if required by state persistence changes.
All user-facing text introduced by this change must be in English.
nonGoals: Do not add any third-party authentication providers (only Internet Identity).
Do not add external databases or non-IC backend services.
Do not implement a multi-canister or microservices architecture.
